title: Falling Rodian

{{Quote|Excuse me, but I believe that the object you are discussing was once the property of Riboga the Hutt. As such, it is now the property of Nirama. We will claim it for him. Give it to us, now.|Jubieck, while in the Fallen Rodian|<ref name="HT" />}}
The '''Falling Rodian''' was a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] situated in the floating [[City/Legends|city]] [[Tolea Biqua]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Genarius]] that operated during the latter [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Regarded as an average bar, the cantina was favored by [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] and known for its regular bar fights. Around [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]], the cantina was the site of a three-way brawl between [[Thaereian military]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], three servants of the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Nirama]] and a group of adventurers for possession of the head of the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[L8O-RC]].

==Description==
[[File:Genarius.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Genarius, the planet on which the Falling Rodian was located]]
The Falling [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] was a [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] that operated in the floating [[City/Legends|city]] [[Tolea Biqua]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Genarius]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Considered as an average, middle-of-the road cantina by the standards of Tolea Biqua, the Falling Rodian was popular with [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] and renowned for its frequent bar brawls. Inside, the cantina had a typical bar setup, including chairs and tables for guests to sit at and an area attended by a [[Bartender/Legends|barkeeper]] from which [[Foodstuff/Legends|drinks]] could be purchased.<ref name="HT" /> Around [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Head Trip]]'' is set during the events of the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign. The ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' establishes that those events commenced one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], thereby placing ''Head Trip'' at around [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]].</ref> the elderly [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Norben]] worked as the bartender of the Falling Rodian and would harass individuals who ventured into the establishment if they did not purchase drinks.<ref name="HT" />

The cantina's customers included Humans and aliens alike and it catered to a variety of tastes. The bar was awash with different sounds and sensations, owing to the various exotic infusions and substances consumed by its clientele, including strangly [[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Ale/Legends|ales]], smouldering [[brazier]]s and hokkums of [[Glitterstim/Legends|Glitterstim]].<ref name="HT" />

Outside the cantina was a sign that depicted an alarmed Rodian falling off the railings of a [[cloud]] city. Beneath the sign was a notice, written in [[Galactic Basic Standard|Basic]] and accompanied by pictographs, which warned that [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weapons and [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were not permitted in the bar. Little was done to enforce these restrictions and the majority of the cantina's patrons were armed.<ref name="HT" />

==History==
{{Quote|Am I glad you came. I've been carrying this around for days, even sleeping with the thing. It's been too much. They're after me. I have to get off the planet.|Ginder, while in the Falling Rodian, desperate to offload head of L80-RC|<ref name="HT" />}}
The Falling Rodian was named to commemorate a particular incident, but the exact nature of the event was eventually forgotten after the cantina subsequently changed ownership about a dozen [[Time/Legends|times]].<ref name="HT" /> Circa [[Galactic Standard Calendar|31 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[Bimm (furred species)/Legends|Bimm]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[Ginder]] came into the possession of the head of [[L8O-RC]], an [[L8O protocol droid|L8O-series protocol droid]], whose [[memory chip]]s recorded of the hiding place of treasures of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Riboga]]. Under threat from various parties who [[Treasure hunting/Legends|sought]] to acquire the head for their own ends, Ginder sought sent a message to a group of adventurers to meet him at the Fallen Rodian, hoping that they could help him out of his predicament. The Bimm ventured to the bar every afternoon as he awaited the adventurers, staying there as long as he could.<ref name="HT" />

Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, the adventurers arrived at the Falling Rodian and waited there for the Bimm information broker to appear. At the same time a trio of thugs searching for the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] head on behalf of the criminal kingpin [[Nirama]]—the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Jubieck (criminal)|Jubieck]] and the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]] [[Orix]] and [[Dimogog]]—also came to the cantina, to drink and discuss business. After the adventurers had been waiting for about an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]], Ginder arrived at the Falling Rodian. The information broker immediately thrust them the droid head and requested that they help him flee Genarius and keep the head out of the hands of the factions who sought it.<ref name="HT" />

{{RPG|''[[Head Trip]]''}}
Three plain [[Clothing/Legends|clothed]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]<ref name="HT" /> of the [[Thaereian military]],<ref name="Thaereian military">In ''Head Trip'', the three [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] that venture to the Falling [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] are identified as [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] troops under the command of [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jir Tramsig]]. The [[Wizards.com]] article ''[[Over Thaere]]'' [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] clarified that Tramsig and his detachment were part of the [[Thaereian military]] and not directly affiliated with the Republic.</ref> another group seeking the protocol droid head, arrived at the bar amid Ginder's meeting with the adventurers. Spotting the droid head, the soldiers claimed it to be stolen and attempted to confiscate it. The confrontation drew the attention of Jubieck and the two Trandoshans, who also also sighted the head and demanded that the adventurers hand it over to them. A three way violent struggle ensured between the adventurers, soldiers and thugs for possession of the head, while Norben hid behind the bar and the cantina's other occupants headed for the exit. Following an explosion outside, which shattered the windows of the Falling Rodian, the head rolled over to the adventurers and they fled the bar with it in their possession.<ref name="HT" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Falling Rodian featured as the main setting of ''[[Head Trip]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure by [[Jeff Grubb]] that was published on [[Wizards.com]] on [[April 29]] [[2001]] as a supplement to [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' roleplaying sourcebook.<ref name="HT" />

In the adventure, the players roleplay as adventurers summoned by Ginder to meet at the Falling Rodian. While the player characters are waiting at the bar for Ginder to arrive, the adventure advises using the "instant cantina generator" feature on Wizards.com to generate random encounters between the players and the bar's denizens. During the bar fight between the different parties competing for the head of L8O-RC, it is possible for the other bar patrons present and bartender Norben to become involved in the fight if the player characters attack them. The adventure does not clarify the cause of the explosion outside the Falling Rodian that shattered the cantina's windows.<ref name="HT" />
